direct_competitors.name: Khanmigo (Khan Academy)

pricing.detail: Free for teachers. Learner plan $4/mo. Parent plan $4/mo. Enterprise/district $10/student/year.

strengths: Safe, monitored AI framework for K-12
Step-by-step tutoring guidance, not just answers
Free for teachers, very affordable for parents
High transparency and safety scores from Common Sense Media
weaknesses: Math answers not trustworthy - incorrect even for basic elementary math
Performance worse for advanced math and physics
Treats partially-knowing students as if they know nothing

traction.reviews: Common Sense Media rated, no G2 reviews

direct_competitors.name: Photomath (Google)

pricing.detail: Free basic features with ads. Premium $9.99/mo for step-by-step solutions and explanations.

strengths: Animated tutorials making abstract concepts concrete
Backed by Google resources since $550M acquisition
weaknesses: Can encourage answer-copying rather than learning

traction.funding: Acquired by Google for ~$550M (2023). Previously raised $29M over 2 rounds.
traction.reviews: 729K+ App Store ratings, 3M+ Google Play reviews

direct_competitors.name: Chegg Study

pricing.detail: Chegg Study $15.95/mo. Chegg Study Pack $19.95/mo with additional features.

weaknesses: Losing to free AI alternatives like ChatGPT

traction.funding: Public company (NYSE: CHGG). Market cap ~$156M (down from $14.7B peak).

direct_competitors.name: Studdy

pricing.detail: Free plan: 30 photo scans/day. Studdy Plus: unlimited scans and chat, extra practice problems.

strengths: Covers math, chemistry, physics, biology, reading
Teaches rather than just giving answers
weaknesses: Relatively small user base vs incumbents

direct_competitors.name: Squirrel AI

pricing.detail: B2B model for learning centers. Pricing not publicly available for individual consumers.

strengths: Patented algorithms for personalized learning paths
Strong presence in China's K-12 market
weaknesses: Valuation concerns ($1B in 2021, employee count dropped from 500 to 41)

direct_competitors.name: Socratic (Google)

pricing.detail: Completely free app by Google. No premium tier.

strengths: Covers middle school to early college
weaknesses: Google may deprioritize in favor of Gemini integration

direct_competitors.name: Mathway (Chegg)

pricing.detail: Free for final answers. Premium $9.99/mo for step-by-step solutions.

strengths: Widest range of math problem types
weaknesses: Free version limited to final answers

direct_competitors.name: Tutor.com (Princeton Review)

pricing.detail: Varies by hours: 1hr/mo $39.99, up to unlimited at $179.99/mo. One-off packages $349.99-$1449.99 for 10-50hrs.

strengths: Combines AI tools with human tutors
AI essay feedback and homework tools
Award-winning platform (2024 Product of the Year)
weaknesses: Very expensive compared to AI-only alternatives
High price may limit K-12 accessibility
AI tools are supplementary, not core

indirect_competitors.name: Varsity Tutors
indirect_competitors.approach: Human tutor marketplace with AI-powered matching and lesson planning tools
indirect_competitors.relevance: Largest US tutoring platform ($107M funding). AI augments human tutors rather than replacing them. If they build a pure AI tutor, they have the distribution.

indirect_competitors.name: Wyzant
indirect_competitors.approach: Human tutor marketplace with 25% platform fee, no AI tutoring component
indirect_competitors.relevance: Represents the human tutoring market the idea aims to disrupt. Mixed reviews (1.9/5 Trustpilot) suggest dissatisfaction that AI could address.

indirect_competitors.name: MagicSchool AI
indirect_competitors.approach: AI platform for teachers (80+ tools for lesson planning, assessment, differentiation), not student-facing tutoring
indirect_competitors.relevance: $65.3M funding, $44.9M revenue, 5M+ educators. Could expand to student-facing AI tutoring given their school district relationships.

indirect_competitors.name: Khan Academy (free content)
indirect_competitors.approach: Free educational videos, exercises, and personalized learning dashboard — not AI tutoring per se
indirect_competitors.relevance: Massive free alternative that sets a floor for pricing. Any AI tutor must justify cost vs free Khan content.

adjacent_competitors.name: Google (Gemini + Photomath + Socratic)
adjacent_competitors.current_focus: General AI assistant, math solving apps, search
adjacent_competitors.pivot_threat: Already owns Photomath ($550M) and Socratic. Could unify into comprehensive AI tutor via Gemini integration.
adjacent_competitors.evidence: Google acquiring Photomath for $550M signals intent in education. Gemini's multimodal capabilities could create an integrated K-12 AI tutor.

adjacent_competitors.name: Microsoft (Copilot in Education)
adjacent_competitors.current_focus: Office 365 for Education, Teams, Copilot integration in productivity tools
adjacent_competitors.pivot_threat: Deep education partnerships with schools and districts. Could embed AI tutoring into existing education infrastructure.
adjacent_competitors.evidence: Microsoft Education partnerships with thousands of school districts. Copilot already being integrated into educational workflows.

adjacent_competitors.name: OpenAI (ChatGPT)
adjacent_competitors.pivot_threat: 89% of students already use ChatGPT for homework. Could launch education-specific mode with guardrails.
adjacent_competitors.evidence: 89% student adoption rate. OpenAI has discussed education use cases. ChatGPT already used as de facto AI tutor despite not being designed for it.

failed_competitors.name: BYJU'S
failed_competitors.reason_failed: Mismanagement, unsustainable acquisitions ($3.63B on 19 companies), poor product quality, financial irregularities. Valuation collapsed from $22B to zero. Filed for bankruptcy in US, insolvency in India.

failed_competitors.name: AllHere Education
failed_competitors.reason_failed: AI chatbot 'Ed' for LAUSD collapsed within 3 months of launch. Company furloughed staff, CEO later indicted for fraud. $6M contract, $12M VC raised.

failed_competitors.name: Chegg (in severe decline)
failed_competitors.reason_failed: AI disruption (ChatGPT) destroyed homework help business model. Stock -99%, revenue -30% YoY, 67%+ workforce cut in 2025. Suing Google over AI Overviews traffic loss.

failed_competitors.name: Tutorspree
failed_competitors.reason_failed: Impractical in-person tutoring pairing model. Could not scale.

market_gaps.gap: AI math accuracy and reliability — AI tutors give incorrect answers even for basic elementary math, with ChatGPT showing ~50% math accuracy
Lack of real teaching and pedagogy — AI tutors help solve problems but students score 17% worse on tests; 70% of teachers say AI weakens critical thinking
No personalized attention or emotional support — AI cannot replicate the human connection and motivational aspects of tutoring
Limited subject coverage — most AI tutors focus on math only, not all subjects
Technical reliability — slow response times, system crashes, unresponsive chatbots
Student engagement and retention — 95% of students opt out of online tutoring software; school districts pivoting back to in-person
Poor customer support and account management across tutoring platforms
